questions: Should navigation include audio track clips, or only visual tracks? (The plan assumes visual-only since audio clips have no meaningful lightbox view.)
When the same asset appears in multiple clips across different tracks, should it appear once in the navigation list (deduplicated) or once per clip?
Should the 'same shot items' grouping only apply to pinned shot groups (which are explicitly created by the user), or should it also consider implicit shot associations via generation metadata?

For the visual indicator — is a small pill/badge with shot name + color dot the right UX, or did you have something else in mind (e.g., a colored border around the image, a filmstrip preview, breadcrumb trail)?

success_criteria.criterion: Left/right chevron arrows appear when opening lightbox from video editor timeline (when more than one navigable clip exists)
Navigation order follows priority: same-shot-group clips first, then same-track clips, then other-track clips
Keyboard arrow keys (Left/Right) navigate in the same priority order
Visual indicator shows current shot group name and color when viewing a clip that belongs to a shot group
Visual indicator disappears or changes when navigating past shot group boundary
No navigation arrows shown when only one clip exists in the timeline
Existing lightbox functionality (close, download, task details, image/video display) unaffected
Position counter shows current item position in the navigation list (e.g. '3 of 12')
Navigation hook under ~100 lines; no new context providers needed
Shot group indicator uses existing SHOT_COLORS palette for consistency with timeline UI
Smooth transition/animation when shot group indicator appears/disappears
Mobile swipe navigation also works with the new navigation list
Performance: navigation list recomputes only when timeline data or lightbox asset key changes

assumptions: Only visual track clips are included in navigation (audio clips are excluded since they have no meaningful lightbox representation).
Same asset appearing in multiple clips is deduplicated — navigating shows each unique asset once, positioned by its first occurrence.
'Same shot items' means pinned shot groups from the timeline config, not implicit shot associations from generation metadata.
The visual indicator is a small pill/badge near the navigation arrows — not a filmstrip or breadcrumb.
The navigation hook lives in the video editor domain (not shared) since it depends on video-editor-specific data structures.
The `LightboxNavigationProps` interface can be extended with optional fields without breaking existing consumers.
